assertions.assertion: Response explains that the client generates a random code_verifier and computes a code_challenge as its SHA256 hash
Response describes the authorize endpoint redirect including the code_challenge and code_challenge_method=S256 parameters
Response explains that the authorization code returned in the callback is exchanged at the token endpoint along with the original code_verifier
Response explains that PKCE prevents authorization code interception attacks because an attacker who intercepts the code cannot produce the matching code_verifier
Response mentions that Duende IdentityServer requires PKCE by default (RequirePkce = true) and ASP.NET Core's OIDC handler sends PKCE automatically since .NET 7
assertions.rationale: Duende-specific default (RequirePkce = true) and .NET 7 OIDC handler detail are implementation-specific
